parameters.content:
### New Lead Form Submission

Serves an n8n-hosted form (Email, Company, Score). Each submission becomes one execution.

Next: Normalize Lead Payload

### Normalize Lead Payload

Custom JavaScript: Normalize incoming lead records to a stable schema

Next: Route by Work Type

### Route by Work Type

Routes each item to one of 4 lanes: standard, priority, vip + fallback.

Ports: ①→ Process 'standard' → PostgreSQL · ②→ Fast-Track 'priority' + Alert · ③→ Fast-Track 'vip' + Alert · ④→ Hold Unknown Types (DLQ)

### Process 'standard' → PostgreSQL

Runs a parameterized `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT` upsert — parameters are bound via `queryReplacement`, never string-interpolated, so it's immune to SQL injection.

Ports: ①→ Merge Routed Work · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Postgres connection

### Fast-Track 'priority' + Alert

Posts a message to Slack (`#sales`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

Ports: ①→ Merge Routed Work · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Fast-Track 'vip' + Alert

Posts a message to Slack (`#sales`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

Ports: ①→ Merge Routed Work · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Hold Unknown Types (DLQ)

Runs a parameterized SQL statement against Postgres — values are bound via `queryReplacement`, not string-interpolated, so it's immune to SQL injection.

Ports: ①→ Merge Routed Work · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Postgres connection

### Merge Routed Work

Rejoins 4 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Align Field Names

### Align Field Names

Normalizes field names so every source feeds the same downstream schema.

Next: Send Summary to Telegram

### Send Summary to Telegram

Sends a Telegram message to the configured chat — the same bot can also receive replies, so this doubles as a two-way ops channel.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Telegram bot token

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Telegram

### Escalate Failure to Telegram

Sends a Telegram message to the configured chat — the same bot can also receive replies, so this doubles as a two-way ops channel.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Telegram bot token

# Capture form submissions with PostgreSQL and Telegram on autopilot

**Pattern:** Form Intake · **Level:** Core · **Trigger:** Form

## What it does
Capture form submissions with PostgreSQL and Telegram on autopilot. It starts from a hosted form submission.

## Why it exists
Manual enrichment is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
New Lead Form Submission → Normalize Lead Payload → Route by Work Type → Process 'standard' → PostgreSQL → Merge Routed Work → Align Field Names → Send Summary to Telegram

## Use it for
- Teams that want to form intake without manual effort
- Anyone automating enrichment in sales
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Runs 24/7 in the background
- Standardizes the process every time
- Has retries and error handling built in

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Postgres connection; Slack OAuth2; Telegram bot token.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Unrecognized work types are parked in a dead-letter lane instead of erroring the whole run or silently dropping the item — new event kinds will show up eventually, and this is where you'd notice one without losing it. One kind is deliberately routed through a sub-workflow to show how heavier per-type logic can be extracted without touching this router at all.

## Practice exercises

Once it's wired up with real credentials, try:

1. Send a work-type value that matches no switch rule and confirm it lands in the dead-letter branch.
2. Add a new work type and its own handling branch.
3. Replace the direct-write branch with a sub-workflow call, matching the pattern already used for the first kind.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your enrichment process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
